---
title: "Universe"
description: "Modelling, trade-offs, and frameworks that cut across domains."
listing:
  contents: "*.qmd"
  type: grid
  grid-columns: 2
  fields: [image, title, summary]
  field-display-names:
    title: "Framework"
    summary: "What it says"
  image-height: "180px"
  sort:
    - "title asc"
  filter-ui: true
  sort-ui: false
  page-size: 50
---

A catch-all section for frameworks that don't fit neatly elsewhere — building good models, constructing 2x2 matrices, navigating impossible trinities.

If you are new here, start with **Building Models** (on why simplification is not a bug but the whole point of a model) and **Three Binding Constraints on Tech Progress** (a framework for diagnosing why a technology isn't spreading as fast as expected). These are the most domain-agnostic frameworks on the site — useful whether you are analysing a policy problem, a business decision, or an intellectual puzzle.
